
### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-20
So then you need to use ->   Option "MetaModes" "1024x768, 1024x768"
and it should work.

### Post by gu014 on 2007-01-20

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-20
You can try it but if i remember right it won't work. Xinerama combines the 2 in to 1 desktop so you end up with 2048x768 as the desktop, but if you have a mismatch size it can't compute it.

### Post by bodhi.zazen on 2007-01-21
Install the nvidia beta driver from here:

[http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)

Be sure to use the **beta driver (Unstable version)**

The unstable version is fine, I have not heard of breakage.

Then in a terminal

nvidia-settings

The new nvidia driver will configure your monitors and twinview :p

See the "X Server Display Configuration" menu ;)

---

### Post by bodhi.zazen on 2007-01-21
Well, at least you have the nvidia driver ;)

Sorry it is not working for you :(

The only other thought I have is for you to add a monitor section manually:

Something like this:
> 

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor 0"
VendorName "Acer"
ModelName "Acer AL1717"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 64.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
    Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor 1"
VendorName "DELL"
ModelName "DELL E193FP"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 64.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
    Option "UseEdidFreqs" "1"
EndSection

Only you will need to put in the correct HorizSync and VertRefresh for both monitors :p

HTH

And here are the relevant sections of my xorg.conf
> Section "Device"
Identifier "twinview"
VideoRam 256000
VendorName "nVidia Corporation"
BoardName "Video device"
Driver "nvidia"
BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 0
Option "TwinView"
Option "MetaModes" "1600x1200,1600x1200"
Option "TwinViewOrientation" "LeftOf"
Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync""UseEdidFreqs"
Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "UseEdidFreqs"
Option "HWCursor" "On"
Option "UseEdidFreqs" "true"
Option "NvAGP" "1"
# Option "NoLogo" "true"
# Option "RenderAccel" "true"
# Option "CursorShadow" "true"
# Option "XvmcUsesTextures" "true"
EndSection

# ************************************************** ********************
# Screen sections
# ************************************************** ********************

Section "Screen"
Identifier "twinviewscreen"
Device "twinview"
Monitor "Generic Monitor"

DefaultDepth 24

Subsection "Display"
Depth 8
Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubsection
Subsection "Display"
Depth 16
Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubsection
Subsection "Display"
Depth 24
Modes "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubsection
Subsection "Display"
Depth 32
Modes "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubsection
EndSection

# ************************************************** ********************
# ServerLayout sections.
# ************************************************** ********************

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "TWINVIEW"
Screen "twinviewscreen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
Endsection

HTH

---

### Post by ghevan on 2007-01-21
checking your original posted xorg.conf file it all seems ok (except for the monitor resolutions, use resolutions both monitors can handle. I don't know if you can use diferent ones) but better to start fresh.

Here is my xorg. 

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Dec 14 16:39:22 PST 2005

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "TwinView Configuration"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"

        # paths to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "i2c"
    Load           "bitmap"
    Load           "ddc"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "latam"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mouse1"
    Option         "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
    Option         "Buttons" "7"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/event4"
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "USB" "on"
    Option         "PressCurve" "88,0,65,85"
    Option         "KeepShape" "on"
    Option         "Twinview" "horizontal"
    Option         "ScreenNo" "2"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/event4"
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "USB" "on"
    Option         "PressCurve" "88,0,65,85"
    Option         "KeepShape" "on"
    Option         "Twinview" "horizontal"
    Option         "ScreenNo" "2"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/event4"
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "USB" "on"
#    Option         "ButtonsOnly" "on"
    Option         "Speed" "2.5"
    Option         "Twinview" "horizontal"
    Option         "ScreenNo" "2"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "COMPAQ MV940"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 85.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 120.0
#    Gamma 1.04 0.925 1.079
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BusID          "PCI:5:0:0"
    Option         "TwinView" "True"
    Option         "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
    Option         "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
    Option         "MetaModes" "1600x1200,1600x1200; 1280x1024,1280x1024; 1600x1200,NULL; 1280x1024,NULL"
    Option         "UseDisplayDevice" "CRT"
    Option         "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "30-85"
    Option         "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "48-160"
    Option          "RenderAccel"           "true"
    Option          "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
    Monitor        "COMPAQ MV940"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Viewport 0 0
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option  "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

```

I followed this 2 references

[http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html](http://www.ublug.org/ubuntu/twinview/twinview-howto-breezy.html)
[http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=85769](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=85769)

Im using Option "UseDisplayDevice" "CRT", but you should use "ConnectedMonitor" "CRT,DFP" for you config. 8in any cas read the explanation of the second link.

hope you figure it out soon =\

### Post by majoridiot on 2007-01-22
try this one:

```
Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen        "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"  
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "Metamodes" "1280x1024, 1280x1024; 1024x768,1024x768; 800x600,800x600; 640x480, 640x480"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
       SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```

it is a functioning twinview xorg.conf that should work for you if everything is ok on your box.
it doesn't have all the video depths, composite, etc. enabled, but should be a good base to work from.

don't worry too much about monitor frequencies, etc. as if they are newer monitors, they should 
detect properly.  also, if you feel the need to play with CRT/DFP definitions, both of your monitors
are considered CRT.  any connection made via the DVI/VGA adapter that is a VGA device is 
considered a CRT.

### Post by ghevan on 2007-01-22
maybe its realted to the metamodes option. try to leave just the option you are going to use like. make it look like this. I think the NULL option is needed ofr the metamodes of just one monitor. 

```
    Option         "MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1280x1024,NULL"
```

Try looking at your system>preferences>screenresolution for the values it gives you. I just have th eoptions I added on my metamodes option. (3200x1200, 2650x1024, 1600x1200 and 1280x1024)

I hope it helps.

### Post by majoridiot on 2007-01-22
what driver version are you running?  i ask because you get this error:

```
(II) Loading sub module "wfb"
(II) LoadModule: "wfb"
(WW) Warning, couldn't open module wfb
(II) UnloadModule: "wfb"
(EE) Failed to load module "wfb" (module does not exist, 0)
```

which seems to be a bug in the driver.  you can regress to driver 8776 (which i know works
with this xorg.conf) or alternately try creating a symlink if you are using driver 9764:

```
ln -s /usr/lib/xorg/modules/nvidia-graphics-1.0-9746/libwfb.so /usr/lib/xorg/modules/
```

(if you try this and it does not work, be sure to delete the symlink in /usr/lib/xorg/modules/)

as described here:
[http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu_Edgy#How_to_install_Beta_Graphics_Driver_.28NVIDIA.29]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu_Edgy#How_to_install_Beta_Graphics_Driver_.28NVIDIA.29")

### Post by majoridiot on 2007-01-22
i would assume the same as well... and that might very well be the problem.  i suggest reverting
to the last stable.  i run dapper, so i'm not sure what the latest edgy stable is, but i would go
with that.  

you will need to comment out alberto's repo that the guide had you add to sources.list first, update
your sources and get the driver.  accoring to that guide:

```
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx nvidia-kernel-common
```

once it's all finished, replace the xorg.conf with the one we've been working with and restart.

if there are problems, Xorg.0.log, of course and note which driver version is installed.

---

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-22
I told you you have to go with the lowest resolution that they both support. since the highest on your acer 1024x768 you need to remove all the other choices. only have the one setting you want to use, don't give the system a choice to use a lower resolution.
( (II) NVIDIA(0): Virtual screen size determined to be 1280 x 1024 )
that means it's trying to run both monitors at 640x480.

I changed my xorg.conf to what it should be for your system. I want you to rename yours to xorg.conf.old and copy the attached one in its place just rename it from xorg.conf.txt to xorg.conf.

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-22
(II) NVIDIA(0): Validated modes:
(II) NVIDIA(0):     "1280x1024,1280x1024"
(II) NVIDIA(0):     "1024x768,1024x768"
(II) NVIDIA(0):     "800x600,800x600"
(II) NVIDIA(0):     "640x480,640x480"

You see this part, don't list the other options, just keep 1280x1024. Here's how it looks in mine->

(II) NVIDIA(0): Validated modes:
(II) NVIDIA(0):     "1280x1024,1280x1024"
(II) NVIDIA(0): Virtual screen size determined to be 2560 x 1024

I don't know what you did but the setup you got now is worse the when you put the first xorg.conf log.

Try this attached xorg.conf if it does not work you have got a monitor or card problem.

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-22
I think somethings screwy with your card, according to that xorg.0.log every things correct, it should be working.

### Post by majoridiot on 2007-01-22
it's probably not a good idea to keep going from conflicting recommendations, so i'll bow out
and let you work with kerry_s... but one mistake you are making is identifying the acer as
"DFP".

per netllama (staff member at nvidia), any monitor connected via the VGA/DVI converter is 
considered a CRT, not a DFP.

in any event, good luck! :D

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-22
> **majoridiot said:**
> it's probably not a good idea to keep going from conflicting recommendations, so i'll bow out
and let you work with kerry_s... but one mistake you are making is identifying the acer as
"DFP".

per netllama (staff member at nvidia), any monitor connected via the VGA/DVI converter is 
considered a CRT, not a DFP.

in any event, good luck! :D

No, that's incorrect, you can see in the xorg.0.log that it is recognized as a dfp. If it were wrong it will tell you and use the correct setting anyways. Been there, done that.

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-22
I'm looking around and i see on this page-> [http://us.download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-9746/README/appendix-g.html](http://us.download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-9746/README/appendix-g.html)
there's this option to set up> nvidia-xconfig --twinview
you want to give that a shot?
also try this part->

    Option "HorizSync"   "CRT-0: 50-110;  DFP-0: 40-70"
    Option "VertRefresh" "CRT-0: 60-120;  DFP-0: 60"

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-01-23
I don't know the odds of 2 bad cards, how old are these cards? I don't know how else to help you, it could be the monitor setup(crt, dfp) maybe the dfp needs some special settings. Also it doesn't help that alot of the info on the net does not pertain to the newer xorg were running these days. Anyways i hope you can get it sorted, i went 2 days(out of 6 years) on 1 monitor and it was driving me nuts, i got to have my dual setup. :wink:  My friend came over and saw it was driving me crazy i kept trying to go to the other monitor(the broken 1 that was off and unplugged, but still sitting there) he went home and dug through his storage and found 1 he was keeping for backup. Then again, it could be your monitor ??
